title: AFRM Q4 Deep Dive: Diversification and Product Expansion in Focus as Growth Continues
text:
AFRM Q4 Results: Key Findings

Publication Date: February 6, 2026

Financial Metrics:
- Revenue: $1.12 billion (29.6% YoY growth, beat estimates by 6.3%)
- Adjusted EPS: $0.72 (13.9% below consensus of $0.84)
- Adjusted EBITDA: $341.7 million (30.4% margin, 9.2% beat)
- Operating Margin: 10.5% (vs. -0.5% prior year)
- Market Cap: $19.61 billion

Core Article Summary:

Affirm's fourth-quarter performance reflected ongoing merchant portfolio expansion and increased transaction activity. The company highlighted several growth drivers: the Affirm Card achieved "nearly doubled" GMV with active cardholders rising 121% year-over-year, while merchant diversification reduced concentration risk through wallet partnerships and new verticals including services and auto repair.

Management indicated that "the business is growing quite well" and emphasized the Affirm Card as a significant expansion engine. The company also promoted its emerging Boost AI tool for merchant optimization, though adoption remains early-stage.

Forward Outlook:

Strategic priorities include accelerating the Affirm Card and international expansion, particularly in the UK market, while pursuing a bank charter application for long-term regulatory stability and future product capabilities. Management plans sustained investment in AI-driven tools and maintaining favorable funding partnerships to support continued growth.
